dataset_title: Upcoming publishing: Population statistics show population size and population changes, such as the number of births, deaths, and immigration and emigration and are available by county, municipality, sex, age, civil status, country of birth and country of citizenship. The statistics are based on data on registered persons that the Swedish Tax Agency supplies to Statistics Sweden.

dataset_title: Administrative information Name and topic Name: Population Topic: Population Next update 20 Responsible division Division for Population Statistics Regional level The nation, county, municipality and basic statistical unit, but for quarterly statistic and estimated populaton regional level is national, county and municipality. Frequency and timeliness Annually. The quaterly statistic is published about 6 weeks after referencetime, which is January 1st, April 1st, July 1st and October 1st. International reporting Not relevant Microdata Data files at the individual level that are processed and stored long-term. The statistic about estimated population and the quaterly statistic are only stored on a short-term basis.

dataset_title: The population is growing despite continued low fertility rates At the end of 2025, 10 605 500 people were registered in Sweden. During the year, the population increased by 17 800 people. The population increase is explained both by more immigrants than emigrants and by the fact that more people were born than died during the year. In 2025, 97 500 children were born, which was the lowest number of births in 23 years. All statistical news for this statistic

dataset_title: Listen Population statistics Upcoming publishing: Population statistics show population size and population changes, such as the number of births, deaths, and immigration and emigration and are available by county, municipality, sex, age, civil status, country of birth and country of citizenship. The statistics are based on data on registered persons that the Swedish Tax Agency supplies to Statistics Sweden. Find on this page Results Key figures Statistical news Tables and graphs Tables in the Statistical Database Publications About the statistics More information Contact Contact Key figures for Sweden Population 10 605 529 Reference period: 0.2 % compared with the same period previous year Statistical news The population is growing despite continued low fertility rates At the end of 2025, 10 605 500 people were registered in Sweden. During the year, the population increased by 17 800 people. The population increase is explained both by more immigrants than emigrants and by the fact that more people were born than died during the year. In 2025, 97 500 children were born, which was the lowest number of births in 23 years. summary: Listen Population statistics Upcoming publishing: Population statistics show population size and population changes, such as the number of births, deaths, and immigration and emigration and are available by county, municipality, sex, age, civil status, country of birth and country of citizenship. The statistics are based on data on registered persons that the Swedish Tax Agency supplies to Statistics Sweden. Find on this page Results Key figures Statistical news Tables and graphs Tables in the Statistical Database Publications About the statistics More information Contact Contact Key figures for Sweden Population 10 605 529 Reference period: 0.2 % compared with the same period previous year Statistical news The population is growing despite continued low fertility rates At the end of 2025, 10 605 500 people were registered in Sweden. During the year, the population increased by 17 800 people. The population increase is explained both by more immigrants than emigrants and by the fact that more people were born than died during the year. In 2025, 97 500 children were born, which was the lowest number of births in 23 years. dataset_title: Background and purpose The statistics show the composition of the population (by sex, age, marital status, and residence) over time. Annual figures for the population by sex, age, marital status and citizenship can be found dating back to January 1st 1976, whereas corresponding figures without citizenship can be found from January 1st 1971. Earlier than that, figures exist from censuses held every ten years, as well as some estimated figures from the intervening years. The statistic on the estimated population at the turn of the year have been in existence since 1970, but only for Norway as a whole. The first estimates at county and municipal level were created on January 1st 2000. The municipal figures are less certain than the county and national figures, and the estimated population in the smallest municipalities will be more uncertain than the estimates for larger municipalities. The statistics on quarterly population changes show population changes over a shorter period of time. The changes include events such as births, deaths and migrations, in addition to a population count. These statistics are connected to the annual statistics on population developments. Starting in 1951, Statistics Norway has produced yearly figures on such developments, based on population register material. We have compiled statistics on the quarterly changes (births, deaths, migrations, and population count) since 1974.

dataset_title: Equal treatment of users No external users have access to the statistics and analyses before they are published and accessible simultaneously for all users on ssb.no at 08 am. This is one of Statistics Norway’s key principles for ensuring that all users are treated equally .

dataset_title: 38) In the 2017-18 Jordan DHS, height and weight data were collected for women aged 15-49 and for children less than 60 months old.
